intro: CISA has begun limited deployment of Anthropic's Mythos AI system for cybersecurity work after months without access to the advanced tool, helping identify security vulnerabilities. Anthropic expanded Claude Cowork to web and mobile platforms, enabling AI agents to complete tasks even when devices are offline as agentic AI expands across office workflows. Gartner warned that over 40% of agentic AI projects risk cancellation by 2027, citing governance, data access, and ROI challenges as primary obstacles.

whitelist.title: A US Cyber Agency Is Finally Using Anthropic’s Mythos
whitelist.desc: CISA went without access to Anthropic’s advanced cyber AI for months, but now a limited number of staff are using it to find plenty of bugs.

whitelist.title: Claude Cowork comes to web and mobile
whitelist.desc: Anthropic's Claude Cowork now runs on web and mobile, letting tasks finish while your laptop is closed, as AI agents push into the whole office.

whitelist.title: Norm Ai raises $120M at a $1.2B valuation
whitelist.desc: AI legal startup Norm Ai has raised a $120M Series C at a $1.2bn valuation, led by Khosla Ventures, and runs its own AI-native law firm, Norm Law.

whitelist.title: Why 40% Of Agentic AI Projects May Be Canceled By 2027
whitelist.desc: Gartner warned that over 40% of agentic AI projects may be canceled by 2027. The problem is governance, data access, ownership and ROI.

The law is known as the Artificial Intelligence Safety Measures Act, and it aims to increase transparency and accountability for large AI models. It requires developers to outline how they assess catastrophic risks and report incidents within specific timeframes. The bill has received support from companies like OpenAI and Anthropic.
